titleStmt.title: Letter from Monsieur J. des Longchamps to Sir Neville Chamberlain, 23 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: J. des Longchamps

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter concerning the travel arrangements for three men who are returning to France from Londonderry and Roscommon to sign up with the French Army.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
CONSULAT DE FRANCE
EN IRLANDE
Dublin le 23rd December 1915 92127C Dear Sir, I beg to
acknowledge with many thanks
the receipt of your letter of the 22nd inst. I have received from the French
Embassy warrants for the free
travelling of the men you refer to,
from Londonderry & Roscommon
to London. I would be very much obliged
to you if you kindly would give Sir Neville Chamberlain Inspector General of
The Royal Irish
Constabulary
The Castle
Dublin
the necessary directions for the
handing of the enclosed
warrant (No 337684) to
Mr Louis Herr, Carrowroe
Park, Roscommon, to travel on the 28h December
next, and for the Police
giving to Louis Herr all
facilities for travelling. Mr Louis Herr should
leave Roscommon by such
a train on the 27th or 28thDecember as to be able to
call at this Consulate, 8
Leinster Street, on Tuesday
28th inst between 11.a.m.
and 1.P.m. to receive the
necessary papers to leave
Ireland. It is indispensable
that he should bring with
him a small photograph
of himself to be stuck on
those papers. Please accept my best
thanks in anticipation Believe me
Yours faithfully LE CONSUL DE FRANCE P.S. J. des Longchamps P.S.We have asked a few days ago,
(the 19h Dec.) Mr Joseph Chenay
45 Rutland Square Dublin
to present himself at this
Consulate to be sent also to
London under the same
circumstances; he has, however,
never presented himself.
J des Long
